post.description: Register a new collaborative user. First user becomes admin, subsequent users require admin privileges.
Authenticate with username and password, receive JWT tokens.
Exchange a refresh token for new access and refresh tokens (token rotation).
Revoke a refresh token.

Consumes the current Basic/JWT session and emits a 60-second JWT usable for the WebSocket handshake. The ticket is ALSO set as `opencode_ws_ticket` HttpOnly+SameSite=Strict cookie so browser WS upgrades can authenticate without exposing the token to JS.

Upgrade opencode to the specified version or latest if not specified.
Write a log entry to the server logs with specified level and metadata.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Filter sessions updated on or after this timestamp (milliseconds since epoch)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Return sessions updated before this timestamp (milliseconds since epoch)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of sessions to return

get.description: Get a list of all OpenCode sessions across projects, sorted by most recently updated. Archived sessions are excluded by default.
Get all available MCP resources from connected servers. Optionally filter by name.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Filter sessions updated on or after this timestamp (milliseconds since epoch)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of sessions to return

get.description: Get a list of all OpenCode sessions, sorted by most recently updated.

post.description: Create a new OpenCode session for interacting with AI assistants and managing conversations.

get.description: Retrieve the current status of all sessions, including active, idle, and completed states.
Retrieve detailed information about a specific OpenCode session.

delete.description: Delete a session and permanently remove all associated data, including messages and history.

patch.description: Update properties of an existing session, such as title or other metadata.

get.description: Retrieve all child sessions that were forked from the specified parent session.
Retrieve the todo list associated with a specific session, showing tasks and action items.

post.description: Analyze the current application and create an AGENTS.md file with project-specific agent configurations.
Create a new session by forking an existing session at a specific message point.
Abort an active session and stop any ongoing AI processing or command execution.
Create a shareable link for a session, allowing others to view the conversation.

delete.description: Remove the shareable link for a session, making it private again.

get.description: Get the file changes (diff) that resulted from a specific user message in the session.

post.description: Generate a concise summary of the session using AI compaction to preserve key information.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of messages to return

get.description: Retrieve all messages in a session, including user prompts and AI responses.

post.description: Create and send a new message to a session, streaming the AI response.

tools.description: @deprecated tools and permissions have been merged, you can set permissions on the session itself now

get.description: Retrieve a specific message from a session by its message ID.

delete.description: Permanently delete a specific message (and all of its parts) from a session. This does not revert any file changes that may have been made while processing the message.
Delete a part from a message

patch.description: Update a part in a message

post.description: Create and send a new message to a session asynchronously, starting the session if needed and returning immediately.

tools.description: @deprecated tools and permissions have been merged, you can set permissions on the session itself now

post.description: Send a new command to a session for execution by the AI assistant.
Execute a shell command within the session context and return the AI's response.
Revert a specific message in a session, undoing its effects and restoring the previous state.
Restore all previously reverted messages in a session.
Approve or deny a permission request from the AI assistant.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of tasks to return

get.description: List all tasks (child sessions) sorted by most recently updated. Optionally filter by parent session or status.
Get detailed information about a specific task including its current status.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of messages to return

get.description: Retrieve all messages from a task session to see its output and progress.

post.description: Cancel a running or queued task.
Resume a completed, failed, blocked, or awaiting_input task with an optional follow-up prompt.

prompt.description: Follow-up prompt to send to the task

post.description: Send a follow-up message to a task session. The task must be in a non-busy state. Returns immediately while the task processes the message.

prompt.description: The follow-up message to send to the task

post.description: Promote a background task to foreground by streaming its session messages. The response streams until the task completes or is cancelled.

get.description: Get an aggregated view of a task and all its child tasks (team members), including cost, status, and file changes for each.
List available OpenCode tools in AnythingLLM Agent Skill format.

post.description: Execute an OpenCode tool by ID. Returns the tool output.
Approve or deny a permission request from the AI assistant.

get.description: Get all pending permission requests across all sessions.
Get all pending question requests across all sessions.

post.description: Provide answers to a question request from the AI assistant.

answers.description: User answers in order of questions (each answer is an array of selected labels)

post.description: Reject a question request from the AI assistant.

get.description: Get a list of all available AI providers, including both available and connected ones.

post.description: Force a refresh of the cached models.dev provider/model catalog, bypassing the freshness TTL. Returns ok:false with an explanatory message if the catalog is managed externally (OPENCODE_MODELS_PATH), fetching is disabled (OPENCODE_DISABLE_MODELS_FETCH), or the fetch itself failed.

get.description: Retrieve available authentication methods for all AI providers.

post.description: Initiate OAuth authorization for a specific AI provider to get an authorization URL.
Handle the OAuth callback from a provider after user authorization.
Start a new collective intelligence debate. Returns the debate report when complete.

get.description: List past debates, most recent first.

put.description: Select the primary synthesis model and parallel participants reused by Debate mode.

get.description: Return the Debate mode model selection reused across sessions.

put.description: Select the primary synthesis model and the parallel debate participants for a session.

get.description: Return the configured debate models for a session, if any.

post.description: Estimate the token and cost budget for a debate configuration.

get.description: Get a debate by ID, including its report if completed.

post.description: Record that a user acted on (or dismissed) specific claims. Used to compute user_action_rate metric.

get.description: Current instance's observability queue/circuit-breaker state. Reflects only the process serving this request, not a global/cross-project view.
Resolved capture policy plus Phase 1 storage disclosure flags for the settings UI (unencrypted local SQLite, no full-content capture available).
Lists local sessions with persisted observability events, independent of the current project directory.

get.summary: List observability events for a session
get.description: Keyset-paginated (ts_ms, id) events for one session, newest first. The session must belong to the current project — a session from another project 404s.
Fetch one event by its ULID. 404s if it doesn't exist or belongs to another project.

get.summary: Get all events in a trace
get.description: The full span sequence for one trace (Timeline/TraceDetail UI), oldest first. Ownership is checked against the first event's session — 404s if the trace doesn't exist or belongs to another project.
Aggregate event counts (by type/status) and total cost for one session. Same ownership check as /events.

get.summary: Get the content-capture opt-in for a scope
get.description: Phase 3 (ADR-1032). Returns the active, non-expired opt-in for one session/project/workspace, or null if none is active. Ownership-checked like every other route in this file.

put.summary: Set the content-capture opt-in for a scope
put.description: Phase 3 (ADR-1032). Grants local_content_redacted or local_full capture for a scope, with a mandatory TTL (max 30 days). Re-opting-in overwrites the previous level/TTL — it never stacks.

post.summary: Revoke the content-capture opt-in for a scope
post.description: Phase 3 (ADR-1032). Immediately stops future content capture for the scope AND clears content already captured on existing events (metadata rows are kept, only local_content_redacted/local_full are cleared).

get.description: Aggregates latency p50/p95, cost per turn, and failure rate by (model_provider, model_id, skill_hmac). Phase 2 feature — returns empty array if insufficient data.

delete.description: Destroys observability events for a scope (session/project/workspace/all). Requires header `X-Confirm-Delete: yes`.

get.description: Streams all matching events as newline-delimited JSON. Supports filtering by session/project/workspace and time window. Returns NDJSON lines.

Aggregate event counts (by type/status) and total cost across a scope. Defaults to the current project when no sessionId/projectId/workspaceId is given — never returns other projects' data implicitly.

get.summary: Phase 4 exporter configuration and last run
get.description: Configured exporters (ADR-1026) — secrets are never returned, only type/host/publicKey — plus the most recent periodic export tick's per-exporter outcome, if any exporter has ever run.

get.summary: Preview the ExportProjection for one event
get.description: Returns exactly what would be sent to a configured exporter for this event — without sending it anywhere. `exportable: false` if the event is not yet a terminal event (shouldExportSpan, ADR-1026) even if it is otherwise valid.

post.summary: Send a synthetic test event through every configured exporter
post.description: Exercises each configured exporter right now with a synthetic, non-real ExportProjection (fake trace/span ids, no data derived from any real event) so credentials/connectivity can be validated without waiting for real traffic or risking real content. Returns per-exporter success/failure, with the same bounded retry policy as the periodic export tick.

get.description: Get list of online users and their current activity.
Streams a JSON document containing all local sessions, messages, and non-secret config. Auth tokens are omitted by default.

delete.description: Destroys all local sessions, messages, auth.json, and user config. Requires header `X-Confirm-Delete: yes`.

get.description: Paginated audit log read (default 100, max 1000).
Search for text patterns across files in the project using ripgrep.
Search for files or directories by name or pattern in the project directory.
Search for workspace symbols like functions, classes, and variables using LSP.
List files and directories in a specified path.

delete.description: Delete a file. 404 if absent; rejects (409) if the file changed since expectedHash.

get.description: Read the content of a specified file.
Get the git status of all files in the project.

post.description: Create a directory (recursive) at the specified path.

get.description: Read raw (untrimmed) text content plus a content stamp for round-trip editing.

post.description: Write text content to a file. Rejects (409) if the file changed on disk since expectedHash. With format=true, runs the formatter and returns the final content.
Rename a file within the project. Rejects (409) if the destination exists or source changed.
Move a file within the project. Rejects (409) if the destination exists or source changed.

Get a list of all available AI agents in the OpenCode system.
Get a list of all available skills in the OpenCode system.

post.description: Install a skill from a URL. Accepts a direct SKILL.md URL or a discovery index URL. The skill is saved to the global ~/.claude/skills/ directory and immediately available.

delete.description: Remove a globally-installed skill by name. Only skills under ~/.claude/skills/ can be removed.

get.description: Get LSP server status
Return all diagnostics from connected LSP servers. Pass ?file= to filter to a single file path.

200.description: Map of file path → diagnostic list

post.description: Get type / documentation hover information at a position in a file.

200.description: Hover result or null when unavailable

post.description: Return source location(s) for the definition of the symbol at the given position.

200.description: List of definition locations (empty when not found)

post.description: Return all references to the symbol at the given position.

200.description: List of reference locations (empty when not found)

post.description: Rename a symbol at the given position across the workspace. Returns a WorkspaceEdit.

200.description: WorkspaceEdit — map of file URI → TextEdit[]

post.description: Return code actions (quick fixes, refactors, source actions) available at the given range.

200.description: List of CodeAction objects (may include WorkspaceEdit and/or Command)

post.description: Execute a workspace command returned by a code action that has no WorkspaceEdit.

200.description: Command result (opaque — varies per LSP server)

post.description: Return completion items at the given position (for autocomplete).

get.description: Return all symbols (functions, classes, variables, etc.) defined in a file. Pass ?file= as an absolute path; the route converts it to a file:// URI internally.

200.description: List of document symbols (DocumentSymbol or flat Symbol)

get.description: Return the working tree status as parsed `git status --porcelain=v1` entries. Each entry includes the XY code so the UI can distinguish staged vs unstaged changes: index (X) is not a space/? → staged; worktree (Y) is not a space/? → unstaged.

post.description: Run `git add` on the given files. Pass an empty `files` array to stage all modified/untracked files (`git add -A`).

files.description: Relative paths to stage; empty = all

post.description: Run `git reset HEAD` on the given files. Pass an empty `files` array to unstage everything.

files.description: Relative paths to unstage; empty = all

post.description: Run `git commit -m <message>`. Returns the short hash of the new commit on success. Returns a 400 if there is nothing to commit or git rejects the operation.

200.description: Short hash of the new commit

post.description: Run `git push <remote> [<branch>]`. Uses system credential helpers — SSH/token auth UI is a separate sub-project. The `ok` field is false and `error` is populated when git exits non-zero.
Run `git pull --rebase <remote> [<branch>]`. Rebases the local branch on top of the remote — keeps history linear. Returns `ok: false` and `error` on failure (e.g. conflicts).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of commits to return

get.description: Return the last N commits in newest-first order. Default limit: 50.

get.summary: Get blame info for a file
get.description: Return per-line blame information using `git blame --porcelain`.
Return all local and remote-tracking branches with current-branch marker.

post.description: Pass `create: true` to run `git checkout -b <name> [<from>]`. Pass `create: false` (default) to switch to an existing local branch via `git checkout <name>`.

create.description: Create new branch if true, switch if false

from.description: Start point for new branch (only used when create: true)

get.description: Returns current git auth configuration with sensitive values masked.

put.description: Saves git auth configuration. Accepts none, https-token, or ssh-key.

get.description: Returns whether a GitHub session is connected (from stored state, no network call) and whether the OAuth app is configured for this build.

post.description: Requests a device/user code pair from GitHub. Call /device/poll on the returned interval.

503.description: GitHub OAuth app not configured for this build

post.description: Call on the interval returned by /device/start (increase it on slow_down). No request body.
Deletes the stored session from whichever backend holds it (keychain / encrypted file / plain file).

Re-validates the GitHub session against the live API and probes git HTTPS with the session's credentials. Distinguishes API reachability from git transport health — never reports 'operational' from the API check alone.

get.description: Unauthenticated probe of the local git installation and HTTPS transport (git --version, exec-path, git-remote-https presence, a read-only ls-remote against a public repo). Safe to run without a GitHub session.
Get formatter status

start.description: Startup script to run when creating a new workspace (worktree)

custom.description: Allow typing a custom answer (default: true)

status.description: Current status of the task: pending, in_progress, completed, cancelled

priority.description: Priority level of the task: high, medium, low

ServerConfig.description: Server configuration for opencode serve and web commands

mdnsDomain.description: Custom domain name for mDNS service (default: opencode.local)

cors.description: Additional domains to allow for CORS

variant.description: Default model variant for this agent (applies only when using the agent's configured model).

description.description: Description of when to use the agent

hidden.description: Hide this subagent from the @ autocomplete menu (default: false, only applies to mode: subagent)

cli_hidden.description: Hide this agent from the terminal CLI agent selector only (Tab cycling and the agent dialog). Does not affect the mobile/web app or programmatic/explicit invocation (default: false).

app_hidden.description: Hide this agent from the desktop/mobile app agent selector only. Does not affect the CLI or programmatic/explicit invocation (default: false).

color.description: Hex color code (e.g., #FF5733) or theme color (e.g., primary)

steps.description: Maximum number of agentic iterations before forcing text-only response

mcp.description: MCP server access control for this agent

allow.description: MCP server names this agent may use

deny.description: MCP server names this agent may NOT use

disabled.description: Disable this variant for the model

enterpriseUrl.description: GitHub Enterprise URL for copilot authentication

setCacheKey.description: Enable promptCacheKey for this provider (default false)

timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds for requests to this provider. Default is 300000 (5 minutes). Set to false to disable timeout.

anyOf.description: Timeout in milliseconds for requests to this provider. Default is 300000 (5 minutes). Set to false to disable timeout.
Disable timeout for this provider entirely.

chunkTimeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds between streamed SSE chunks for this provider. If no chunk arrives within this window, the request is aborted.

command.description: Command and arguments to run the MCP server

environment.description: Environment variables to set when running the MCP server

enabled.description: Enable or disable the MCP server on startup

timeout.description: Timeout in ms for MCP server requests. Defaults to 5000 (5 seconds) if not specified.

clientId.description: OAuth client ID. If not provided, dynamic client registration (RFC 7591) will be attempted.

clientSecret.description: OAuth client secret (if required by the authorization server)

scope.description: OAuth scopes to request during authorization

url.description: URL of the remote MCP server

enabled.description: Enable or disable the MCP server on startup

headers.description: Headers to send with the request

oauth.description: OAuth authentication configuration for the MCP server. Set to false to disable OAuth auto-detection.

timeout.description: Timeout in ms for MCP server requests. Defaults to 5000 (5 seconds) if not specified.

$schema.description: JSON schema reference for configuration validation

urls.description: URLs to fetch skills from (e.g., https://example.com/.well-known/skills/)

snapshot.description: Enable or disable snapshot tracking. When false, filesystem snapshots are not recorded and undoing or reverting will not undo/redo file changes. Defaults to true.

share.description: Control sharing behavior:'manual' allows manual sharing via commands, 'auto' enables automatic sharing, 'disabled' disables all sharing

autoshare.description: @deprecated Use 'share' field instead. Share newly created sessions automatically

autoupdate.description: Automatically update to the latest version. Set to true to auto-update, false to disable, or 'notify' to show update notifications

disabled_providers.description: Disable providers that are loaded automatically

enabled_providers.description: When set, ONLY these providers will be enabled. All other providers will be ignored

model.description: Model to use in the format of provider/model, eg anthropic/claude-2

small_model.description: Small model to use for tasks like title generation in the format of provider/model

default_agent.description: Default agent to use when none is specified. Must be a primary agent. Falls back to 'build' if not set or if the specified agent is invalid.

username.description: Custom username to display in conversations instead of system username

provider.description: Custom provider configurations and model overrides

instructions.description: Additional instruction files or patterns to include

auto.description: Enable automatic compaction when context is full (default: true)

prune.description: Enable pruning of old tool outputs (default: true)

reserved.description: Token buffer for compaction. Leaves enough window to avoid overflow during compaction.

cost_cap.description: Cumulative USD cost cap per task session. Further /task/:id/followup calls return 429 cost_cap_exceeded once reached. Undefined = no cap.

max_parallel.description: Maximum number of background tasks executing concurrently per project. Additional tasks are enqueued and started when slots free. Default: 4.

openTelemetry.description: Enable OpenTelemetry spans for AI SDK calls (using the 'experimental_telemetry' flag)

observability.description: Native local observability: metadata-only event capture, no prompts/responses, no network unless exporters is explicitly configured.

enabled.description: Enable native local observability event capture (default: false). See ADR-1020 through 1031.

captureMode.description: Capture level for observability events. Phase 1 never persists readable prompts, responses, tool args/output, or raw error messages regardless of mode.

retentionDays.description: Delete events older than this many days. Undefined keeps events until another retention limit applies.

maxEvents.description: Maximum local observability event count. Default: 100000.

exporters.description: Phase 4 optional exporters (e.g. Langfuse). Each exporter only ever receives a redacted ExportProjection (ADR-1026), never raw event content or Phase 3 opt-in text. Empty/undefined by default: no network calls happen until an exporter is explicitly configured here.

host.description: Langfuse instance base URL, e.g. https://cloud.langfuse.com

backfillOnStart.description: When true, the first export tick that finds a configured exporter exports the ENTIRE existing event history instead of only events inserted from that point forward. Default: false (no backfill). Only takes effect once at least one exporter is configured — has no effect while exporters is empty.

primary_tools.description: Tools that should only be available to primary agents.

continue_loop_on_deny.description: Continue the agent loop when a tool call is denied

mcp_timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds for model context protocol (MCP) requests

sandbox.description: Sandboxed execution environment for bash commands

mount_workdir.description: Mount the project directory into the container

rag.description: RAG system for semantic code search and cross-session memory

enabled.description: Enable RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) for semantic code search

provider.description: Embedding/search provider (bm25 works offline with zero config)

api_key.description: API key for embedding provider (falls back to provider's default key)

top_k.description: Number of results to inject into context

dlp.description: Data Loss Prevention - redacts secrets, keys, and tokens from content sent to LLM providers

enabled.description: Enable DLP (Data Loss Prevention) to redact secrets before sending to LLM

scan_tool_outputs.description: Run secret + prompt-injection scanner on tool outputs before passing them to the LLM. Off by default to avoid false positives in dev.

policy.description: Policy engine for conditional permission rules beyond allow/deny/ask

enabled.description: Enable policy engine for conditional permission rules

max_edit_lines.description: Warn when edits exceed this many lines (default: 500)

match.description: Regex pattern to match against file paths or commands

collaborative.description: Collaborative multi-user mode with JWT auth, presence, and session sharing

require_auth.description: Require authentication for all API requests

jwt_secret.description: Secret for JWT signing. Auto-generated if not set.

lsp_memory.description: Memory management for LSP servers (idle timeout, max concurrent, LRU eviction)

idle_timeout_minutes.description: Shut down LSP servers after this many minutes of inactivity

max_concurrent.description: Maximum number of LSP servers running simultaneously

max_memory_mb.description: Maximum RSS memory per LSP server in MB (restart if exceeded)

crash.description: Crash reporter — local-first, optional remote upload

upload_endpoint.description: Opt-in HTTPS endpoint where crash reports will be POSTed as JSON. Default: undefined (local file only).

fallback.description: Cascading provider fallback. 'local' = on cloud error, retry via local-llm-server. 'cloud' = on local error, retry via configured cloud provider. Default: null (disabled).

fallback_cloud_providerID.description: Override the secondary provider used when fallback='cloud'. Must match a providerID declared in `provider`. Recommended: a fast, cheap model (e.g. anthropic/claude-haiku, google/gemini-flash). Default: null (= first configured non-local provider).

enabled.description: Enable audit log. Records session create/delete, auth mutations, tool permission grants, task cancellations, config writes.

retention_days.description: Days of audit entries to retain

ws_auth_legacy.description: Allow the legacy `?authorization=Bearer+<jwt>` query-string WS handshake. Default true in Sprint 4 for backward compat; flip to false once all clients migrate to the cookie/Sec-WebSocket-Protocol flow.

anythingllm.description: AnythingLLM integration for document RAG and cross-platform AI

workspaces.description: Workspace slugs to search. Searches all workspaces if omitted.

inject_context.description: Auto-inject relevant documents from AnythingLLM into system prompt

expose_tools.description: Expose OpenCode tools as AnythingLLM Agent Skills via HTTP API

vector_bridge.description: Use AnythingLLM as an additional vector store for RAG search

collective.description: Collective Intelligence / Blind Spot Hunter configuration

default_tier.description: Default debate tier when not specified

max_budget_usd.description: Global cost cap per debate in USD

enable_canary.description: Enable canary injection on Deep tier

enable_memory.description: Seed new debates with past blind spots

model.description: Ollama model for shadow analysis (default: llama3.2)

divergence_threshold.description: Keyword divergence ratio to trigger alert

ab_mode.description: Enable silent A/B testing on 10% of Tier 2+ debates

retention_days.description: Days to retain debate history before GC

startCommand.description: Additional startup script to run after the project's start command

code.description: Two-character XY status code from git status --porcelain=v1
